title: ipomoea prainii
intro: Ipomoea prainii, also known as the Blue Peaflower or Thai Edible Flower, is a type of flowering vine native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its leaves and flowers. The health benefits of Ipomoea prainii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support cardiovascular health. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed fresh or dried, and is also used in traditional Thai cuisine as a garnish and edible flower.
